message:
feat(terminal): implement raw mode, capabilities detection, and control

This commit adds the terminal abstraction layer providing raw mode
control via termios FFI, terminal capability detection, cursor
management, and mouse protocol handling.

src/terminal/mod.rs - Terminal State:
Terminal<W: Write> struct:
- writer: Buffered output
- capabilities: Detected terminal features
- in_alt_screen: Alternate buffer state
- mouse_enabled: Mouse tracking state
- cursor_visible: Cursor visibility
- cursor_x, cursor_y: Current position

Public Functions:
- terminal_size() -> (u16, u16): Get terminal dimensions
- is_tty(fd) -> bool: Check if file descriptor is TTY
- enable_raw_mode() -> RawModeGuard: Enter raw mode with RAII

Terminal Methods:
- enter_alt_screen(), exit_alt_screen()
- enable_mouse(), disable_mouse()
- show_cursor(), hide_cursor()
- set_cursor_position(x, y)
- set_cursor_style(shape, blink)
- write_ansi(sequence): Output escape sequence
- flush(): Flush buffered output

src/terminal/raw.rs - Raw Mode (termios FFI):
RawModeGuard struct:
- original_termios: Saved terminal settings
- Implements Drop for automatic restoration

enable_raw_mode():
- Gets current termios via tcgetattr
- Clears flags: ICANON (line buffering), ECHO (character echo)
- Clears ISIG (disable Ctrl+C/Z signal handling)
- Sets VMIN=1, VTIME=0 for non-blocking reads
- Returns RawModeGuard for RAII cleanup

disable_raw_mode():
- Restores original termios via tcsetattr
- Called automatically by RawModeGuard::drop()

Safety Notes:
- Module-level #![allow(unsafe_code)] for FFI
- All unsafe blocks have safety comments
- libc::tcgetattr/tcsetattr are well-defined
- Terminal always restored on panic via RAII

src/terminal/capabilities.rs - Feature Detection:
ColorSupport enum:
- TrueColor: 24-bit RGB (\x1b[38;2;r;g;bm)
- Color256: 256-color palette (\x1b[38;5;nm)
- Color16: Basic 16 colors (\x1b[30-37m, \x1b[90-97m)

Capabilities struct:
- color_support: Detected color depth
- terminal_id: Optional terminal identifier
- has_mouse: Mouse tracking support
- has_focus: Focus in/out events
- has_kitty_keyboard: Enhanced keyboard protocol
- pixel_width, pixel_height: Optional pixel dimensions

Detection Sources (in priority order):
1. COLORTERM env var: "truecolor" or "24bit"
2. TERM env var: "256color" suffix
3. Terminal queries (if supported)
4. Fallback: Assume TrueColor for modern terminals

src/terminal/queries.rs - Terminal Queries:
Query Sequences:
- DA1 (Primary Device Attributes): \x1b[c
- DA2 (Secondary Device Attributes): \x1b[>c
- XTVERSION: \x1b[>0q
- Pixel dimensions: \x1b[14t
- Kitty keyboard: \x1b[?u

Response Parsing:
- parse_da1_response(): Extract capabilities
- parse_da2_response(): Get terminal version
- parse_xtversion(): Get terminal name/version
- parse_pixel_size(): Extract pixel dimensions

Query Protocol:
- Send query sequence
- Read response with timeout
- Parse and extract information
- Update capabilities struct

src/terminal/cursor.rs - Cursor Control:
CursorShape enum:
- Block: Full cell highlight
- Underline: Line under text
- Bar: Vertical line (I-beam)

CursorState struct:
- visible: bool
- shape: CursorShape
- blink: bool
- x, y: Position (0-based)

Operations:
- show(), hide(): Visibility control
- set_shape(shape, blink): Appearance
- move_to(x, y): Position
- move_relative(dx, dy): Relative movement
- save(), restore(): DEC save/restore

src/terminal/mouse.rs - Mouse Protocol:
MouseMode enum:
- None: Disabled
- Button: Click events only
- Motion: Motion while pressed
- Any: All motion events

MouseProtocol enum:
- X10: Legacy 8-bit encoding
- SGR: Extended encoding (preferred)

Operations:
- enable_tracking(mode, protocol)
- disable_tracking()
- parse_event(bytes): Parse mouse input

SGR Format: \x1b[<btn;x;y;M or m
- btn: Button number with modifiers
- x, y: 1-based coordinates
- M: Press, m: Release

X10 Format: \x1b[Mbxy
- b: Button + 32
- x, y: Coordinate + 32 (limited to 223)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
